design_philosophy.core_loop: Pilot spacecraft through scrolling stages, collect weapon chips to power up, manage health-through-weapon-level resource, defeat stage bosses and final mechanoid sphere
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Weapon level management as dual health/offense system; players must balance powering up while staying alive when hit, as damage degrades weapon level rather than separate health
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Ace pilot commanding an advanced fighter craft (Super Aleste) equipped with transformable weaponry to stop an invading mechanical sphere and its reinforcements
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Integrates weapon upgrade level directly into survivability mechanic—damage reduces weapon power, forcing strategic weapon choice and upgrade prioritization; eight distinct weapon types with manipulable functions create high experimentation ceiling; 'Short Game' mode emphasizes score chasing over progression

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives with checkpoint restart; convertible to Special Lives (one-time resurrections at exact death location) via power-up collection
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint-based recovery on standard death; Special Lives bypass checkpoint system for zero-progress loss
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player controls weapon selection and upgrade prioritization; weapon function manipulation (Shot-Control button) allows tactical adaptation to situations; choice to convert lives to Special Lives affects difficulty pacing

progression.upgrade_system: Eight weapon types with six-tier power levels; orange chips increment level (higher levels require more chips); green chips grant instant level-up; weapon switching via numbered items
progression.unlock_mechanism: Weapons selected via numbered pickups during gameplay; no permanent unlocks between runs
replayability_hooks: Short Game mode with stage segments focused on scoring
Multiple difficulty levels (harder, very hard, hardest) with expanded narrative content in Japanese version
Eight distinct weapons with manipulable functions encourage different playstyle approaches
Score-attack emphasis in Short Game variant

world_building_techniques: Opening exposition: mechanical sphere invades Earth in 2048, destroys military defenses, hovers over South American jungle drawing Nazca-like patterns
Character-driven narrative in Japanese version: pilot Raz and alien co-pilot Thi (prisoner aboard sphere with reality-warping powers)
Difficulty-gated story reveals: Japanese version expands ending across three higher difficulty tiers
Version-specific narrative: Japanese version contains full character backstory and mystery resolution; American/European versions strip character presence and ending content

boss_design.philosophy: Stage-culminating mechanical and organic threats; boss patterns require weapon adaptation and precise positioning given health-through-weapon-level system
notable_bosses: Final mechanical sphere (final stage; multi-phase invasion boss representing primary threat)
Reinforcement units summoned from deep space (stages vary by version)

faction_indicators: Super Aleste pilot corps (represents human technological response)
Mechanoid sphere faction (extraterrestrial invader with autonomous defense)
danger_signals: Mechanical sphere expanding and drawing Nazca-pattern energy from jungle (resource extraction/colonization metaphor)
Destruction of major world cities (existential threat scale)
Sphere's autonomous defense system immune to military attack (power asymmetry)
environmental_storytelling: South American jungle setting becomes battleground (geographic specificity grounds sci-fi threat)
Nazca Line visual parallel (ancient mystery invoked by modern invasion)
Level design variations across environment types (jungle, space, mechanical interiors implied)
